blocks:
Surah Qul Yā Ayyuhā al-Kāfirūn

And it is named Surah al-Jahd (الجحد) (The Denial). It is Makkan, though it is narrated from Ibn ‘Abbās and Qatādah that it is Madinan. It consists of six verses by consensus.* بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ*** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) قُلْ يا أَيُّهَا الْكافِرُونَ. (1) Say, 'O faithless ones!

(٢) لا أَعْبُدُ ما تَعْبُدُونَ. (2) I do not worship what you worship,

(٣) وَلا أَنْتُمْ عابِدُونَ ما أَعْبُدُ. (3) nor do you worship what I worship.

(٤) وَلا أَنا عابِدٌ ما عَبَدْتُّمْ. (4) Nor will I worship what you have worshipped,

(٥) وَلا أَنْتُمْ عابِدُونَ ما أَعْبُدُ. (5) nor will you worship what I worship.

(٦)** لَكُمْ دِينُكُمْ وَلِيَ دِينِ ** (6) To you is your religion, and to me is my religion.'

You do not abandon it, nor do I abandon it. In al-Amālī: A group of Quraysh intercepted the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, among them were ‘Utbah ibn Rabī‘ah, Umayyah ibn Khalaf, al-Walīd ibn al-Mughīrah, and al-‘Āṣ ibn Sa‘d. They said: "O Muhammad, come, let us worship what you worship, and you worship what we worship, so that we and you may share in the matter. If what we are upon is the truth, then you will have taken your share of it, and if what you are upon is the truth, then we will have taken our share of it." So Allah the Almighty revealed the Surah.

It was said regarding the reason for the repetition that the first [negation] relates to the future, for لَا (lā) only precedes the imperfect verb (مُضَارِع) when it signifies the future, and the second [negation] relates to the present or what has passed.

And al-Qummī [narrated]: Abū Shākir al-Dayṣānī asked Abū Ja‘far al-Aḥwal about that, saying: "Does the Wise One speak such a statement and repeat it time after time?" Al-Aḥwal had no answer regarding this, so he entered Madinah and asked al-Ṣādiq عليه السلام about it. He said: "The reason for the revelation of the verse and its repetition was that Quraysh said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله: 'You worship our god for a year, and we worship your God for a year, and you worship our god for a year, and we worship your God for a year.' So Allah answered them with the like of what they said." (The narrative continues.)

In Thawāb al-A‘māl and al-Majma‘, narrated from him عليه السلام: "Whoever recites قُلْ يا أَيُّهَا الْكافِرُونَ (Say, 'O faithless ones!') and قُلْ هُوَ اللهُ أَحَدٌ (Say, 'He is Allah, the One!') in an obligatory prayer (فَرِيضَة)—Allah will forgive him, his parents, and his offspring. And if he was wretched (شَقِيّاً), he will be erased from the register of the wretched (دِيوَان الْأَشْقِيَاء) and established in the register of the fortunate (دِيوَان السُّعَدَاء). And Allah will grant him life as a fortunate one, cause him to die as a fortunate one, and resurrect him as a martyr."

And in al-Majma‘ and al-Kāfī, narrated from him عليه السلام, he said: "My father used to say: قُلْ يا أَيُّهَا الْكافِرُونَ (Say, 'O faithless ones!') is a quarter of the Quran."

And it is added in al-Majma‘: And when he finished it, he would say: "I worship Allah alone" (أَعْبُدُ اللهَ وَحْدَهُ).

And in it [al-Majma‘] and al-Qummī, narrated from him عليه السلام: "When you finish it, say: 'My religion is Islam' (دِينِيَ الْإِسْلَام) three times."
